compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Why
The documented contract (one reconvergence impl shared by CLI/library/MCP; remote pushes
visible within <=1 min; one throttle marker with no double-fetch; `REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off` /
`REBAR_NO_SYNC=1` / `--no-pull` opt-outs) is only tested via config translation and a
dispatcher-passthrough assertion that patches `ensure_fresh`
(`tests/unit/test_config_sync_aliases.py`, `test_no_stall.py`). No test proves remote
visibility or the shared-throttle no-double-fetch **across all three facades**. VALIDATED
(partial: single impl is real, but the parity guarantees are untested).

## What
Add a two-clone parity test with a counted fetch seam exercising all three facades.

## Scope
- New test(s) over `reads.py` `ensure_fresh` + the throttle marker + CLI/library/MCP entry.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Push a remote update; after marker expiry each of CLI, library, MCP sees it.
- [ ] Repeated reads within the throttle window produce exactly one fetch total.
- [ ] Each opt-out (`REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off`, `REBAR_NO_SYNC=1`, `--no-pull`) keeps the remote
      update invisible while local replay still works.
- [ ] A fetch failure yields consistent local state (no stall/corruption) in each facade.
- [ ] `make test` green.

file_impact.reason: ensure_fresh + throttle marker under test; reference only
new two-clone CLI/library/MCP parity + throttle + opt-out test

compiled_state.title: Three-facade read-freshness parity: remote visibility, shared throttle, and opt-outs
